what_it_does: This Python script scans a hardcoded project directory for Markdown files, sends each file's first 3000 characters to a local HTTP embeddings endpoint (BAAI/bge-m3), and appends relative-path/embedding records to a JSONL file, resuming from prior output and logging progress.
observations: Hardcodes ROOT to a WSL path under /mnt/c/Users/Workstation001/Documents/Claude/Projects/Visual Designer
Outputs to ~/wizard-run-1/embed.jsonl and resumes by reading existing 'rel' values
Uses urllib to POST to http://localhost:8001/v1/embeddings with model BAAI/bge-m3
Reads only the first 3000 characters of each file and skips files with fewer than 30 non-whitespace characters
Finds files via a bash subprocess using find and grep -vE for node_modules and .git
Retries failed embedding requests up to 3 times with 1-second sleeps
standouts: Tightly couples the script to a specific local Windows/WSL project path
Directly calls a localhost embedding service without authentication
Implements a simple incremental/resumable JSONL pipeline
conventions: Top-level script with no function main or CLI arguments
Uses subprocess for file discovery rather than os.walk
Basic retry loop with fixed sleep
concerns: Hardcoded absolute paths, model name, and API URL make the script non-portable
No authentication or API key handling for the embedding endpoint
Silent truncation of file content may lose semantic signal
16 concurrent localhost requests could overload the local server
Resume logic only checks relative path, not content hash or modification time
bash find command constructs a regex string without escaping special characters in ROOT
notes: A quick, hardcoded, resumable local-batch embedding generator for Markdown files in a specific Visual Designer project folder.
questions: Why is the input truncated to 3000 characters rather than chunked?
Why are only Markdown files indexed?
What embedding server is expected at localhost:8001?
Is 16 workers optimal for the local server?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears intended to build an embedding index of project Markdown files, which matches what it does.
intention_signals: It is trying to create a reusable, resumable embedding corpus of project Markdown notes so they can be searched or retrieved semantically using a local BGE-M3 model.
contribution: Provides a concrete, self-contained, resumable, parallelized local embedding pipeline that produces a JSONL mapping of Markdown file paths to dense vectors.
summary_for_embedding: A Python script that walks a hardcoded project directory, sends each Markdown file's first 3000 characters to a local BAAI/bge-m3 embedding endpoint, and appends relative-path/vector records to a JSONL file with resume and progress logging.
intention_for_embedding: To create an incremental, parallel embedding index of project Markdown documentation for downstream retrieval or semantic search.

symbols.does: Reads a file and fetches its embedding from a local HTTP endpoint with retries.
symbols.description: The emb function computes the file's path relative to ROOT, reads up to 3000 UTF-8 characters while ignoring decode errors, skips files shorter than 30 non-whitespace characters, and POSTs the truncated text to a local OpenAI-compatible embeddings API. It retries up to three times and returns a record containing the relative path and either the embedding vector or an error/skip marker.
